---
title: "Quantitative Development"
subtitle: "Research systems, econometrics, financial analytics, and reproducible implementation"
---

## Focus

This site documents a quantitative development agenda at the intersection of:

- macroeconometrics;
- quantitative finance;
- financial stability;
- banking analytics;
- reproducible research systems;
- R, Python, SQL, C++, and Quarto workflows.

## Core implementation areas

::: {.callout-note}
The objective is not only to publish analysis, but to show how analytical products are engineered: data structure, code quality, reproducibility, model diagnostics, documentation, and deployment.
:::

### 1. Econometric systems

Time series, structural breaks, local projections, VAR/VECM, GARCH-X, DCC-GARCH, Bayesian VAR, and regime-switching models.

### 2. Quantitative finance

Monte Carlo simulation, exotic options, risk measures, portfolio analytics, backtesting, and C++ acceleration.

### 3. Research infrastructure

Versioned data pipelines, automated reporting, reusable R/Python functions, Quarto publishing, and Git-based workflows.

### 4. Public technical writing

Short, rigorous, implementation-oriented posts for quantitative development and applied research.
